spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: 1 buttons / 1 inputs / 1 selects / 2 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5156290162720768 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: manual adaptation logic side-by-side with the built-in one
openQuestions.detail: The rtcdegradationpreference-enum-value-maintain-framerate-and-resolution feature's headline framing — WebRTC: new degradation-preference value that disables internal video adaptation, letting the app adapt manually — calls out "manual adaptation logic side-by-side with the built-in one". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: what happens under congestion when no adaptation runs
openQuestions.detail: The rtcdegradationpreference-enum-value-maintain-framerate-and-resolution feature's headline framing — WebRTC: new degradation-preference value that disables internal video adaptation, letting the app adapt manually — calls out "what happens under congestion when no adaptation runs". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: custom-adaptor under rtcdegradationpreference-enum-value-maintain-framerate-and-resolution: 3 pass / 3 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
